hero_dek: Every day, fresh science from arXiv becomes text anyone can read. Here's how it all works — before you dive in.
whatis_lead: Every day arXiv — the largest open library of new science — publishes hundreds of papers. We take their full text, hand it to an AI, and ask for one honest thing: explain it clearly. Not shorter, not louder — just clear.
whatis_muted: Every article comes out in several languages and at four depths. All free, non-commercial, forever. We adapt a paper only when its license allows it.
depth_intro: Not "short" and "full", but a ladder. Start wherever you're comfortable and go deeper if it grabs you. The depth switch sits at the top of every article.
rung1_when: When the topic is new and you want to grasp the essence without jargon. Any jargon here is always spelled out.
rung2_desc: Once science already draws you in.
rung2_when: A lively account with intuition and imagery. The default level — every article opens here.
rung3_desc: With formulas and the story of the discovery.
rung3_when: Closest to the original. For those who want depth and aren't afraid of math.
rung4_when: One thought, like a post in a feed. To decide whether to read on.
map_intro: Left to right — from the feed to a living map of knowledge. The logo always brings you home.
map_main: A feed of articles by day. An endless stream grouped by date — like a news feed for science.
map_tags: Concepts. Topics in physics (black holes, dark matter…). Click for every article on the topic plus its explanation.
map_laws: Laws and principles. The "house of formulas" — laws, theorems, effects. A formula is only a rendering; the meaning is in the text.
map_scientists: Scientists. The great minds behind the discoveries — with a biography and related topics.
map_sections: arXiv sections. Cosmology, exoplanets, high-energy physics… — a filter by field.
map_authors: Authors. Researchers who publish on arXiv. An A–Z index, with tag chips beside each name.
map_graph: The knowledge graph. Tags ⇄ laws ⇄ scientists and all their links. You can wander through knowledge, not just read it.
map_theory: Labs. Interactive "benches": the Doppler effect, a heat engine, a gas, a kettle — turn the parameters with your own hands.
map_fav: Favorites. Articles you've marked with a heart, gathered in one place.
langs_intro: The language switch is in the row under the menu. Russian is the source language; the rest are prepared by AI.
langs_note: The Arabic version is complete, with a right-to-left (RTL) layout: the same frontier science, in the same plain words. The architecture doesn't cap the number of languages — a new one is added without rewriting the site.
search_h2: Three ways to find what you need
search_intro: The 🔍 button in the header. Plain text searches articles, and three prefixes go straight to the point:
search_examples: For example, #black_hole — articles on the topic, @Hawking — by author, !Einstein — linked to a scientist. Next to it, the 📅 Calendar: year → month → day — jump back to any date. And the sections filter narrows the feed to the arXiv fields you want.
graph_h2: Wander through knowledge, don't just read it
graph_p: Every article links to topics; topics link to laws and scientists; scientists link back to topics. It's one knowledge graph. On any tag, law, or scientist page there's a mini-map of its surroundings — with a depth dial + / −. And on the graph page — the whole map at once, with filters and search across all three types.
graph_note: The practical point: you came for one article about neutron stars — and left understanding how they connect to general relativity, to the effect that describes it, and to the people who discovered it.
tiles_kicker: Nice things to know along the way
tile1_s: Toggles and remembers — read whichever way is easy on the eyes.
tile2_s: A short intro to the site — opens on click.
tile3_s: Every article has an honest estimate of how long it takes.
tile4_s: Real figures and plots from the PDF, with captions, plus an AI cover.
tile5_s: Mark an article or leave a short note — no sign-up.
tile6_s: The button at the bottom takes you onward, not back to the feed.
foot_tag: science in plain words · for everyone · free
contact_cadence: For now we cover the top 10 papers a day, with a focus on physics. Next comes the full stream, unfiltered.
contact_authors: Are you an author? Write to us from the address listed in your paper and we'll correct the summary.
contact_write: Liked it, or didn't — tell us:
